title: Secondary Analysis of Electronic Health Records
description: This is the first book published that describes the process of curating, exploring and analyzing messy electronic health record data. Like a cookbook, it comes with SQL and R codes and queries to demonstrate each step using the publicly available Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care (MIMIC), a database of patients admitted to the intensive care units of the Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center. The open-access book has been downloaded close to 1.7 million times since its publication in 2016, and has been translated to Spanish, Mandarin, Portuguese and Korean.

title: MIMIC-III Database
description: MIMIC-III, a freely accessible critical care database. This paper is a description of the MIMIC-III dataset. It has been cited more than 5000 times since its publication in 2016. The database contains de-identified health data associated with over 40,000 patients who stayed in critical care units of the Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center between 2001 and 2012.

title: MIT Laboratory for Computational Physiology
description: Visit the MIT Laboratory for Computational Physiology website to learn more about our mission, team, and ongoing research projects in healthcare AI and computational physiology.
